summary: The diff shows backend/main.mo has been modified to use stable var declarations with Map.empty<K,V>() (from mo:core/Map) for all major state variables. This addresses REQ-2's requirement to avoid non-stable HashMaps. However, the diff is truncated and does not show the preupgrade/postupgrade hooks that REQ-1 explicitly requires. The approach using mo:core/Map with stable var may satisfy stable-compatibility if Map from mo:core is a stable type, but the absence of preupgrade/postupgrade hooks is a concern for REQ-1. Additionally, the build request specifically called for HashMap-to-stable-array patterns with Iter.toArray, which is not visible in the diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: A system func preupgrade() hook must serialize all non-stable collections to their stable backing arrays before upgrade
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows no preupgrade function. The AC explicitly requires this hook to be present.

missing_requirements.requirement: A system func postupgrade() hook must restore all non-stable collections from their stable backing arrays after upgrade
missing_requirements.reason: No postupgrade function is visible in the diff. The AC explicitly requires this hook.

missing_requirements.requirement: All HashMaps are backed by stable entry arrays populated in preupgrade and used to reconstruct maps in postupgrade using Iter.toArray pattern
missing_requirements.reason: The diff uses mo:core/Map directly as stable vars rather than the HashMap-to-stable-array pattern with Iter.toArray and reconstruction in postupgrade as specified in the AC. No stable backing arrays or Iter.toArray usage is visible.

hallucinated_features.description: Use of mo:core/Map and mo:core/Set (a new core library) instead of the standard base HashMap pattern
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request specifies converting HashMaps using stable backing arrays with Iter.toArray and preupgrade/postupgrade hooks. The diff instead imports from mo:core/Map and mo:core/Set which were not mentioned in the build request, representing a different architectural approach than what was specified.

hallucinated_features.description: Import of AccessControl, Storage, and MixinStorage modules
hallucinated_features.reason: The diff introduces imports for authorization/access-control and blob-storage modules that were not part of the stable storage fix requirements.
